TF-E6.Summary: MVP 6: Production — Supply Chain, MITRE, Cache Warming, Deployment Gates
TF-E6.Proof: proof/mvp6/; 207 pytest passed; docs/SUPPLY-CHAIN-SECURITY.md, DEPLOYMENT-GATES.md, GOVERNANCE.md; ai-bom.yaml + pip-audit CI; MITRE 91.7% coverage
TF-E6.Description:
Production hardening: AI-BOM, OpenSSF Scorecard CI, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, prompt cache warming, deployment gates with canary rollout, agent config signing, governance runbooks, and RAG quarantine stub.

SCOPE:
- infrastructure/ai-bom.yaml with weekly validation
- OpenSSF Scorecard ≥7.0 policy, pip-audit blocking CI
- MITRE ATT&CK for AI Systems coverage >80%
- PromptCacheWarmer background task for high-traffic agents
- Deployment gates: staging canary, smoke tests, gradual prod rollout
- Cryptographic signing of agent config manifests
- Emergency procedures and governance documentation
- RAG poisoning defense stub if vector search enabled

EFFORT: L (45 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure, Security, CI/CD
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E5 complete

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E6-production

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ Supply Chain, § Observability)
- docs/SUPPLY-CHAIN-SECURITY.md (when created)
- docs/DEPLOYMENT-GATES.md (when created)
- docs/GOVERNANCE.md (when created)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- ai-bom.yaml validates in CI; pip-audit blocks critical CVEs
- MITRE coverage doc shows >80% applicable techniques
- Cache hit rate >70% under load test with warming enabled
- Docker images signed with Cosign in CI
- Canary deployment documented and tested in staging

Create AI Bill of Materials manifest and supply chain security documentation.

USE CASES:
- Security auditor reviews ai-bom.yaml for all models and libraries in production
- CI fails when runtime model not listed in BOM
- Weekly BOM freshness check warns if stale >7 days

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create infrastructure/ai-bom.yaml: models (gpt-5.5, claude-opus-4-8, gpt-4o-mini, llama-3.1, prompt-guard-2), libraries from pyproject.toml
- Create docs/SUPPLY-CHAIN-SECURITY.md: BOM purpose, update cadence, ownership
- Create backend/security/bom.py: load_ai_bom(), validate_bom_freshness()
- Create scripts/validate_ai_bom.py CLI
- Wire CI step: uv run python scripts/validate_ai_bom.py

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure, Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E5 complete

TESTING CRITERIA:
- validate_ai_bom.py exits 0 on match
- Missing model in BOM exits 1
- test_ai_bom.py passes

EDGE CASES:
- Local LLM disabled: still listed with security_posture=self-hosted
- Stale BOM >7 days: CI warn
- Dev dependency excluded from production BOM section

TF-E6.Summary: OpenSSF Scorecard and pip-audit CI Gates
TF-E6.Description:
Integrate pip-audit and OpenSSF Scorecard policy into CI with documented thresholds.

USE CASES:
- PR with critical CVE in dependency fails CI
- Scorecard policy requires SECURITY.md and Dependabot
- Manual scorecard run documented for weekly audit

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add pip-audit to .github/workflows/ci.yml backend job
- Block on critical/high CVEs in production deps
- Create SECURITY.md at repo root if missing
- Document Scorecard ≥7.0 threshold in docs/SUPPLY-CHAIN-SECURITY.md
- Dependabot or Renovate config in .github/dependabot.yml
- test_supply_chain_ci.py for policy constants

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: CI/CD
DEPENDENCIES: TF-055

TESTING CRITERIA:
- pip-audit passes on current lockfile
- SECURITY.md exists
- CI fails on injected fake critical CVE (test script)

EDGE CASES:
- CVE without fix: ADR exception with expiry
- Scorecard CLI not in CI: manual weekly, pip-audit still blocks
- npm audit parallel job for frontend

TF-E6.Summary: MITRE ATT&CK for AI Systems Coverage Mapping
TF-E6.Description:
Map TaskFlow AI defenses to MITRE ATT&CK techniques with >80% coverage target.

USE CASES:
- Security team reviews MITRE coverage doc for audit
- Each technique maps to detection control and test reference
- Gaps documented with remediation timeline

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/security/MITRE-ATTACK-COVERAGE.md
- Map: Prompt Injection → InputSecurityScanner + Spotlighting (95%)
- Map: Tool Poisoning → MCPResponseValidator (90%)
- Map: Excessive Agency → delegation + allowlists (85%)
- Include coverage %, detection module, test file reference
- Link from docs/SECURITY.md

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Documentation, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-048

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Document lists >=10 applicable techniques
- >80% marked as covered
- Each covered technique has test reference

EDGE CASES:
- N/A techniques (model theft): explicitly marked N/A with rationale
- New MITRE version: document review date
- Uncovered technique: ticket reference for future work

Implement background PromptCacheWarmer to maintain >70% cache hit rate during peak hours.

USE CASES:
- Peak hours 8am-6pm: cache warmed every 4 minutes per agent
- First user AI request after idle does not pay full cold-cache latency
- Ops monitors cache hit rate gauge stays >70%

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/llm/cache_warmer.py: PromptCacheWarmer class per proposal
- warm_cache(agent_id): minimal tokens ping with cached system prompt
- keep_warm() asyncio background task on app startup
- Settings: CACHE_WARMING_ENABLED, CACHE_TTL=300
- Skip low-traffic agents <1 req/min
- Document in docs/OBSERVABILITY.md and prompts/AGENT.md

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-039

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Warmer populates cache (mock API shows cache_read on second call)
- Disabled via settings skips background task
- last_warmed timestamp updated per agent
- Load test: hit rate >70% with warmer vs <50% without

EDGE CASES:
- API key invalid: warmer logs error, doesn't crash app
- All agents warmed concurrently: stagger by 10s
- Off-peak hours: reduce frequency to 15 min
- Prompt version change: force re-warm

Define and implement deployment pipeline with staging canary, smoke tests, and gradual production rollout.

USE CASES:
- Merge to integration triggers staging deploy with smoke tests
- Canary receives 10% traffic before full prod promotion
- Failed smoke test blocks production promotion

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/DEPLOYMENT-GATES.md: gate checklist per environment
- Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml: staging → smoke → canary → prod
- Smoke tests: /health, /metrics, POST /ai/parse-task mock, auth flow
- GHCR push on merge to epic/taskflow-implementation
- Rollback procedure documented
- infrastructure/AGENT.md updated

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure, CI/CD
DEPENDENCIES: TF-056, TF-012

TESTING CRITERIA:
- deploy.yml dry-run succeeds
- Smoke test script exits 0 against staging
- Rollback documented with <15 min RTO target

EDGE CASES:
- Staging Supabase separate from prod
- Smoke test flake: retry 3x before fail
- Canary metrics regression: auto-rollback trigger documented
- Secrets via GitHub environments

Cryptographically sign agent config manifests; reject unsigned configs at runtime.

USE CASES:
- CI signs prompts/manifest.json after prompt pack changes
- Runtime loader verifies signature before agent startup
- Tampered agent config rejected with ConfigSignatureError

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create infrastructure/agent-manifest.json: agent ids, prompt versions, tool allowlists
- Sign with Cosign or ed25519 key in CI (COSIGN_PRIVATE_KEY secret)
- backend/kernel/config_loader.py: verify_signature() before loading allowlists
- Public key in repo; private key in CI only
- Document rotation in docs/SUPPLY-CHAIN-SECURITY.md

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Security, Infrastructure
DEPENDENCIES: TF-038, TF-056

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Valid signature loads config
- Tampered manifest fails verification
- Unsigned manifest fails in production APP_ENV=production
- Dev mode allows unsigned with warning

EDGE CASES:
- Key rotation: support two public keys during transition
- Manifest missing agent: startup fails fast
- Signature on wrong file version: version field in manifest

Create governance documentation and incident response runbooks for production operations.

USE CASES:
- On-call engineer follows incident-response-playbook for injection spike
- Security dwell time breach triggers 15-min response SLA procedure
- Emergency AI kill switch disables /api/v1/ai/* via feature flag

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create docs/GOVERNANCE.md: roles, change management, review cadence
- Create docs/security/incident-response-playbook.md
- Settings: AI_FEATURES_ENABLED global kill switch
- Document vendor reassessment schedule (6 months)
- Emergency procedure: disable MCP, disable AI, rotate JWT secret

EFFORT: M (4 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Documentation
DEPENDENCIES: TF-057

TESTING CRITERIA:
- AI_FEATURES_ENABLED=false returns 503 on AI endpoints
- Playbook covers injection spike, DLQ flood, credential compromise
- GOVERNANCE.md references all security docs

EDGE CASES:
- Kill switch during in-flight graph: cancel with timeout
- Partial disable: per-workspace AI flag future scope
- Post-incident: lessons.md template in playbook

TF-E6.Summary: MVP 6 Production E2E Proof and Docker Signing
TF-E6.Description:
Cosign Docker image signing, production E2E proof package, and final PLAN.md update.

USE CASES:
- Every production image signed and verifiable with cosign verify
- proof/mvp6/ contains load test, security scan, deployment checklist
- Portfolio-ready deployed hybrid stack documented

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Add Cosign sign step to deploy.yml after docker push
- Create proof/mvp6/: pytest output, load test cache hit rate, cosign verify log, checklist.md
- Optional: RAG quarantine stub in backend/memory/quarantine.py if vector search flag set
- Update docs/PLAN.md all epics TF-E1–TF-E7
- Final README: deploy links Vercel + GHCR backend

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Infrastructure, Testing
DEPENDENCIES: TF-055, TF-056, TF-057, TF-058, TF-059, TF-060, TF-061

TESTING CRITERIA:
- cosign verify passes on built image
- proof/mvp6/checklist.md all items checked
- Full pytest suite green
- Cache hit rate proof >70% under load test

EDGE CASES:
- Cosign key unavailable in fork PR: sign only on main branch
- Load test without API keys: use mock LLM provider
- RAG not implemented: quarantine stub behind FEATURE_VECTOR_SEARCH=false
